items.hook: Lord is a Japanese manga series written by Buronson and illustrated by Ryoichi Ikegami.
items.body: It was serialized in Shogakukan's seinen manga magazine Big Comic Superior from September 2004 to February 2011, with its chapters collected in 22 tankōbon volumes. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: House of Representatives, representing Wisconsin's 2nd congressional district for the 56th and 57th congresses (1899–1903).
items.body: Herman Bjorn Dahle was an American businessman and Republican politician from Dane County, Wisconsin. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: In Face of the Verdict is a 1936 detective novel by John Rhode, the pen name of the British writer Cecil Street.
items.body: It is the twenty fourth in his long-running series of novels featuring Lancelot Priestley, a Golden Age armchair detective. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Vertical is a 2010 novel by Rex Pickett and the second novel in the Sideways Series.
items.body: It is a sequel to the novel Sideways, which was made into a successful 2004 film of the same name. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: It is located along the Tampere–Haapamäki Line, where Kolho railway station is located.
items.body: Kolho is a village in the city-municipality of Mänttä-Vilppula in Finland. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Cricket on the Hearth: A Fairy Tale of Home is a novella by Charles Dickens, published by Bradbury and Evans, and released 20 December 1845 with illustrations by Daniel Maclise, John Leech, Richard Doyle, Clarkson Stanfield and Edwin Henry Landseer.
items.body: Dickens began writing the book around 17 October 1845 and finished it by 1 December. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
items.body: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

Actor who was the first Irish woman to win an Oscar for her role in the 1989 film classic My Left Foot

Brenda Fricker, who has died aged 81, was only the second Irish actor – and the first female one – to win an Oscar, for her role

items.hook: Traction City is a novella by Philip Reeve and is a prequel to the Mortal Engines Quartet.
items.body: It was released as a flip book alongside Chris Priestly's teachers' tales of terror for World Book Day. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Robert Peston found it 'too difficult' returning to Wales after his wife's death
items.body: The couple spent a lot of time in Wales, enjoying walks along the coast.
